entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: Tropical germplasm of Zea mays L. used in the study

entities.name: POST FLOWERING STALK ROT
entities.description: A major biotic constraint to maize production in tropical and sub-tropical environments

entities.name: FUSARIUM MONILIFORME
entities.description: A major pathogen causing severe yield losses and Fusarium stalk rot in the Asian tropics

entities.name: MACROPHOMINA PHASEOLINA
entities.description: A major pathogen causing severe yield losses and Macrophomina stalk rot in the Asian tropics

entities.name: ASIAN TROPICS
entities.description: The geographic region where post flowering stalk rot causes severe yield losses

entities.name: FUSARIUM STALK ROT
entities.description: A specific type of post flowering stalk rot caused by Fusarium moniliforme

entities.name: MACROPHOMINA STALK ROT
entities.description: A specific type of post flowering stalk rot caused by Macrophomina phaseolina

entities.name: RESISTANCE TO POST FLOWERING STALK ROT
entities.description: The genetic ability of maize to withstand post flowering stalk rot

entities.name: DIALLEL MATING DESIGN IV
entities.description: A mating design used to study gene action and estimate combining ability of inbred lines

entities.name: GENE ACTION
entities.description: The mechanism of gene expression and inheritance for resistance to stalk rots

entities.name: COMBINING ABILITY
entities.description: The capacity of inbred lines to produce superior hybrids

entities.name: GENERAL COMBINING ABILITY
entities.description: The average performance of a parent in a series of hybrids

entities.name: SPECIFIC COMBINING ABILITY
entities.description: The deviation of a hybrid's performance from the expected value based on GCA

entities.name: ARTIFICIAL INOCULATION
entities.description: The process of intentionally introducing pathogens to evaluate disease resistance

entities.name: BAKER RATIO
entities.description: A ratio used to determine the relative importance of GCA over SCA

entities.name: ADDITIVE GENE EFFECT
entities.description: A type of gene action where the effect of alleles is cumulative, inferred from the Baker ratio

relationships.target: RESISTANCE TO POST FLOWERING STALK ROT
